records.ety: [L. neglectus, p. p. of neglegere (negligere) to disregard, neglect, the literal sense prob. being, not to pick up; nec not, nor (fr. ne not + -que, a particle akin to Goth. -h, -uh, and prob. to E. who; cf. Goth. nih nor) + L. legere to pick up, gather. See No, adv., Legend, Who.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Neglected; p. pr. & vb. n. Neglecting.]

defs: Not to attend to with due care or attention; to forbear one's duty in regard to; to allow to pass unimproved, unheeded, undone, etc.; to omit; to disregard; to slight; as, to neglect duty or business; to neglect to pay debts.
quotes: I hope My absence doth neglect no great designs.
This, my long suffering and my day of grace, Those who neglect and scorn shall never taste.

defs: To omit to notice; to forbear to treat with attention or respect; to slight; as, to neglect strangers.

Omission of proper attention; avoidance or disregard of duty, from heedlessness, indifference, or willfulness; failure to do, use, or heed anything; culpable disregard; as, neglect of business, of health, of economy.
quotes: To tell thee sadly, shepherd, without blame, Or our neglect, we lost her as we came.

defs: Omission of attention or civilities; slight; as, neglect of strangers.
The state of being disregarded, slighted, or neglected.
quotes: Rescue my poor remains from vile neglect.
